customer_anxiety_profile: Gary is not anxious — he is self-motivated and re-engaged after a missed appointment. His primary risk profile is low urgency ('six months, I don't care') and a tendency to self-resolve rather than push back, which means the closer should not mistake his agreeableness for commitment. He is conversational and detail-oriented; the closer should match his tempo and let him finish his thoughts.
unresolved_concerns: Security system requirement: Gary stated he wants a security system installed and has been shopping for one. Ion does not offer this. The closer needs to acknowledge this cleanly and redirect — Gary self-resolved during the setter call, but the expectation was never formally addressed.

Age-based savings skepticism: Gary raised 'I'm 82, what am I gonna save it for?' — the closer should be prepared to reframe value around principle and not-wasting-money rather than long-term savings ROI.
No urgency: Gary explicitly said 'next month, six months, I don't care.' The closer needs to create a reason to move forward without applying pressure that could alienate a self-motivated customer.
sensitive_topics_raised: Gary is 82 years old and retired — value framing should center on principle and monthly bill reduction, not long-term investment return.
Gary is conversational and detail-oriented (airport navigation story, security system specifics, electrical distribution setup) — the closer should allow space for Gary to talk and not rush the pace.
exact_promises_rep_made: Joel will send a text confirming the appointment with his name and the time.
closer_should_know: Gary wants solar on outbuildings as well as the main house, has a centralized electrical distribution point on the property, and has already discussed battery storage with a prior solar rep. This is a multi-structure inquiry with battery interest — the proposal scope is broader than a standard single-home install. Capture the outbuilding details early and flag the battery conversation; Gary has thought about this more than a typical first-touch customer.
